title: Parmigiana
description: Baked aubergine with tomato sauce and mozzarella.
steps: Slice and salt the aubergines, then let them drain and dry.
Coat in flour, egg and breadcrumb mixture, then bake until golden.
Make a quick tomato sauce with garlic, tomatoes, vinegar and basil.
Layer aubergine, sauce, mozzarella and Parmesan, then bake until browned.

title: Pad Thai
description: Vegetarian pad thai with tofu, vegetables and peanuts.
steps: Soak the noodles and prep the vegetables, tofu and sauce.
Stir-fry shallot, garlic and tofu with the eggs, then remove from the pan.
Fry the carrot and mushrooms, add noodles and sauce, then toss everything together.
Finish with bean sprouts, spring onions, coriander, peanuts and lime.

title: Asian Cucumber Salad
description: This Asian Cucumber Salad is cool and refreshing and pairs well with many things. It's vegan and gluten-free and is a nice addition to meals and bowls you are already making.

ingredients.name: Soy sauce (or gluten-free liquid amino acids)
Chili paste, chili flakes, or sriracha (optional)

steps: Score the cucumber with the tines of a fork before slicing.
Optional: Salt the sliced cucumbers and place them in a strainer for 20 minutes or up to a few hours to release liquid. Discard the liquid.
Chop scallions. You can also use chives, cilantro, or Thai basil.
Combine the strained cucumbers (if salted), chopped scallions, seasoned rice vinegar, toasted sesame oil, soy sauce (or liquid amino acids), and sweetener in a salad bowl.
Grate fresh ginger into the salad bowl.
Grate fresh garlic into the salad bowl.
Optional: Add red chili paste, chili flakes, or sriracha for heat.
Mix all ingredients thoroughly and adjust salt and spice level to your liking.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds before serving.

title: Creamy Pasta Gorgonzola Recipe With Mushrooms
description: If you love gorgonzola cheese or are a fan of blue cheese in general youÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂre going to love this pasta gorgonzola with a rich creamy gorgonzola sauce! Gorgonzola is my favorite cheese or at least one of my favorite cheeses (I have many). But you can make this easy vegetarian recipe with another blue cheese if you prefer.
steps: Make the gorgonzola sauce by combining heavy cream, milk, and 100g gorgonzola cheese.
Cook the pasta al dente in a large pot of salted water, reserving some pasta water before draining. Drain the pasta and toss with olive oil to prevent sticking.
In a large skillet, bake the mushrooms on medium heat in olive oil and butter until golden brown. Optionally, add a little white wine to the pan.
Add the gorgonzola sauce to the skillet with the mushrooms and mix well.
Add the cooked pasta to the pan and toss everything together.
Add a splash of the reserved pasta water to achieve a creamy gorgonzola pasta sauce.
Serve the pasta gorgonzola topped with chopped walnuts, fresh parsley, and black pepper.

title: Veggie burritos
description: Avocado, tomatoes and coriander Latin American style

ingredients.name: kidney beans, canned, drained and rinsed

steps: To make the salsa, combine the red onions, tomatoes, vinegar, half the parsley and half the oil, then season to taste. Toss the avocado slices with a splash of the lime juice, and set aside.

Heat the remaining oil in a non-stick pan over a medium-high heat and cook the onions for 5 mins, then stir in the grated carrot and mushrooms, and cook for 3-5 mins more until lightly browned. Mix in the tomato purÃÂ©e and spices, and cook for 3-5 mins until the purÃÂ©e turns dark red. Pour in 200ml water, the kidney beans, corn and remaining parsley. Cook for 5-6 mins until the mixture has reduced, thickened and is heated through. Season with the cayenne pepper and salt and pepper to taste.

Warm the tortillas following pack instructions and reheat the rice until piping hot. Assemble the burritos by topping the tortillas with the lettuce, vegetable filling and rice. Dot over the avocado slices and soured cream, then fold in one side of the tortillas and roll up. Serve warm, with the salsa on the side.

title: Creamy courgette lasagne
description: Enjoy a vegetarian lasagne bursting with spinach and courgette, held together with a creamy mascarpone sauce. It's a filling midweek meal for all the family
steps: Heat oven to 220C/fan 200C/gas 7. Put a pan of water on to boil, then cook the lasagne sheets for about 5 mins until softened, but not cooked through. Rinse in cold water, then drizzle with a little oil to stop them sticking together.

Meanwhile, heat the oil in a large frying pan, then fry the onion. After 3 mins, add the courgettes and garlic and continue to fry until the courgette has softened and turned bright green. Stir in 2/3 of both the ricotta and the cheddar, then season to taste. Heat the tomato sauce in the microwave for 2 mins on High until hot.

In a large baking dish, layer up the lasagne, starting with half the courgette mix, then pasta, then tomato sauce. Repeat, top with blobs of the remaining ricotta, then scatter with the rest of the cheddar. Bake on the top shelf for about 10 mins until the pasta is tender and the cheese is golden.

title: Korean bibimbap
description: Bibimbap is a Korean dish of warm rice topped with sautéed seasoned vegetables decorating the top. These vegetables are usually individually cooked and seasoned to ensure maximum flavour and colour from each vegetable. In this recipe, we still use a variety of vegetables but they’re steamed to make things quicker - feel free to substitute for any other vegetables, just make sure to cut them finely so that they all have a similar cooking time. For extra flavour and freshness, you could also add a handful of rocket, watercress or other salad vegetables too.

ingredients.name: sesame oil, plus extra to serve
sesame seeds, plus extra to serve

steps: Mix the bibimbap sauce ingredients together in a small bowl with 1 tbsp water and set aside.

Put the sliced mushroom, courgette, carrot and beansprouts on a plate. Put a small heatproof bowl or ramekin upside down in the middle of a medium lidded pan. Add enough water to come halfway up the ramekin and bring to a boil. Carefully put the plate of vegetables on top of the ramekin and put a lid on the pan. Steam the vegetables for 3 mins until tender.

Meanwhile, put the spinach in a bowl and pour boiling water over the top. Stir to wilt the leaves, then drain and rinse under cold water. Squeeze any excess water from the spinach, then mix in a bowl with the sesame oil, a pinch of salt and sesame seeds. Set aside.
Heat the vegetable oil in a frying pan over a high heat. Crack in the egg and fry to your liking.

Fill a bowl with the rice. Top with the fried egg, then use tongs to add the steamed vegetables around it. Serve with the bibimbap sauce, a little extra sesame oil and sesame seeds. Mix everything together before eating.
